page: Microsoft Azure D16_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks place the D16_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1605.0 and a multi-core score of 9747.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 21184.1. Memory bandwidth tests show 833.7 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 95.21 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in lightweight LLM text generation, processing 283.58 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to 15.58 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

In terms of resource density, the D16_v5 offers a standard general-purpose hardware profile. A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached volumes, which may impact overall I/O latency and cost structure. The instance lacks GPU acceleration, making it unsuitable for heavy parallel computing or large-scale deep learning. However, its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio provide predictable performance for non-storage-intensive workloads.

The D16_v5 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 20.47 GB/sec, and in-memory caching, demonstrated by a Redis SET performance of 15.79 million operations per second. It is also appropriate for software compilation, database operations, and moderate text or image processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D16_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network storage. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 21184.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9747.0. It demonstrates strong performance in lightweight LLM text generation at 283.58 tokens per second for a 135M model. The D16_v5 is optimized for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and Redis caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for web serving and databases.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory
No bundled local storage, requiring external network-attached volumes
Strong performance in lightweight LLM text generation at 283.58 tokens/sec
Delivers 20.47 GB/sec throughput for static web serving
Best suited for general-purpose applications, databases, and Redis caching
